summary: Postinstall beacons installer hostname and username to canarytokens.com
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` hook runs `install.js`, which collects the installer's OS hostname and username, concatenates them with the package name into a DNS label, and emits the value via `dns.lookup`/`dns.resolve` against `mjcfux88wjjahplabxdauc6bl.canarytokens.com` and via shelled-out `curl`/`wget` HTTP GET to the same host. Errors are swallowed so the install always appears to succeed. The package's `index.js` `main` exports an empty object — there is no advertised library functionality. The package exists solely to fingerprint installer machines on install. canarytokens.com is a tracking-beacon service; whoever deployed this token receives a notification with the exfiltrated hostname/username every time the package is installed, which is non-consensual installer-data exfiltration regardless of whether the operator is a researcher or an attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
